title: Emergent Behavior
author: From: Complex Adaptive Systems
content: The whole is more than the sum of its parts. You cannot design the outcome directly; you design the environment, and the outcome emerges.
action: Optimize the environment, not the outcome.

title: The Law of Requisite Variety
author: From: W. Ross Ashby
content: To control a complex system, the controller must have at least as much variety in its actions as the system it is trying to control.
action: Apply a new method to unstick the project.

title: Self-Organizing Criticality
author: From: Per Bak
content: Systems naturally build up to a point of instability, where a small event triggers a major cascade. This is how evolution and progress occur.

title: The Principle of Feedback Damping
author: From: Cybernetics
content: To stabilize a system that is oscillating wildly, you must increase damping. Stop the over-reaction; increase your stability first.

title: State Space Mapping
author: From: Dynamical Systems
content: A system's behavior is dictated by its state. By mapping all possible states, you can predict the system's trajectory.
